summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several key developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine has unveiled new systems including the Gnome-NS ground “mother drone” for carrying FPVs, the Ptashka strike quadcopter capable of flying on glass thread, and the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone, while also destroying a Russian Ka-27 helicopter with an FPV drone launched from a naval drone—marking a first in military history. On the defensive side, Ukraine’s I-SEE system tracks Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and the updated Gretz XL electronic warfare system from Ukrainian BlueBird will protect against Mavic drones. Russia has deployed knockoff versions of Ukraine’s Vampire heavy bomber drones, with the 81st Brigade already shooting down five, and a Russian T-80BVM tank equipped with an anti-drone grill survived 18 Ukrainian FPV drone strikes. Additionally, Ukraine’s $880M drone line has turned Russian frontline movement into a “death sentence” in just 12 months, and Motor-G is now shipping 200,000 drone motors per month, making it Ukraine’s largest engine manufacturer by a wide margin.
